result.markdown:
---
title: "SNV & Indel Calling"
subtitle: "Step 2: Detecting single nucleotide variants and small insertions/deletions"
sidebar: wes
---

## Overview

Variant calling identifies differences between the sample and reference genome. This includes SNVs (single nucleotide variants) and indels (insertions/deletions).

## Germline Variant Calling

### GATK HaplotypeCaller


::: {.cell}

```{.bash .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Call variants per sample
gatk HaplotypeCaller \
    -R reference.fa \
    -I sample.bqsr.bam \
    -O sample.g.vcf.gz \
    -ERC GVCF \
    --native-pair-hmm-threads 4 \
    -L targets.interval_list

# Joint genotyping (for cohorts)
gatk GenomicsDBImport \
    -V sample1.g.vcf.gz \
    -V sample2.g.vcf.gz \
    -V sample3.g.vcf.gz \
    --genomicsdb-workspace-path genomicsdb \
    -L targets.interval_list

gatk GenotypeGVCFs \
    -R reference.fa \
    -V gendb://genomicsdb \
    -O cohort.vcf.gz
```
:::


### Variant Quality Score Recalibration (VQSR)


::: {.cell}

```{.bash .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Build SNP recalibration model
gatk VariantRecalibrator \
    -R reference.fa \
    -V cohort.vcf.gz \
    --resource:hapmap,known=false,training=true,truth=true,prior=15.0 hapmap.vcf.gz \
    --resource:omni,known=false,training=true,truth=false,prior=12.0 omni.vcf.gz \
    --resource:1000G,known=false,training=true,truth=false,prior=10.0 1000G.vcf.gz \
    --resource:dbsnp,known=true,training=false,truth=false,prior=2.0 dbsnp.vcf.gz \
    -an QD -an MQ -an MQRankSum -an ReadPosRankSum -an FS -an SOR \
    -mode SNP \
    -O snp.recal \
    --tranches-file snp.tranches

# Apply SNP recalibration
gatk ApplyVQSR \
    -R reference.fa \
    -V cohort.vcf.gz \
    -O cohort.snp_recal.vcf.gz \
    --recal-file snp.recal \
    --tranches-file snp.tranches \
    -mode SNP \
    --truth-sensitivity-filter-level 99.5

# Repeat for indels
gatk VariantRecalibrator \
    -R reference.fa \
    -V cohort.snp_recal.vcf.gz \
    --resource:mills,known=true,training=true,truth=true,prior=12.0 Mills.vcf.gz \
    -an QD -an MQRankSum -an ReadPosRankSum -an FS -an SOR \
    -mode INDEL \
    -O indel.recal \
    --tranches-file indel.tranches

gatk ApplyVQSR \
    -R reference.fa \
    -V cohort.snp_recal.vcf.gz \
    -O cohort.final.vcf.gz \
    --recal-file indel.recal \
    --tranches-file indel.tranches \
    -mode INDEL \
    --truth-sensitivity-filter-level 99.0
```
:::


## Somatic Variant Calling

### Mutect2 (Tumor-Normal Pair)


::: {.cell}

```{.bash .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Create Panel of Normals (recommended)
# First, run Mutect2 in tumor-only mode on each normal sample
for NORMAL in normals/*.bam; do
    SAMPLE=$(basename ${NORMAL} .bam)
    gatk Mutect2 \
        -R reference.fa \
        -I ${NORMAL} \
        -max-mnp-distance 0 \
        -O pon/${SAMPLE}.vcf.gz
done

# Create PoN
gatk CreateSomaticPanelOfNormals \
    -R reference.fa \
    -V pon/normal1.vcf.gz \
    -V pon/normal2.vcf.gz \
    -V pon/normal3.vcf.gz \
    -O pon.vcf.gz

# Run Mutect2 on tumor-normal pair
gatk Mutect2 \
    -R reference.fa \
    -I tumor.bam \
    -I normal.bam \
    -normal normal_sample_name \
    --germline-resource gnomad.vcf.gz \
    --panel-of-normals pon.vcf.gz \
    -L targets.interval_list \
    -O tumor_vs_normal.vcf.gz \
    --f1r2-tar-gz f1r2.tar.gz

# Learn read orientation model (for FFPE artifacts)
gatk LearnReadOrientationModel \
    -I f1r2.tar.gz \
    -O read-orientation-model.tar.gz

# Calculate contamination
gatk GetPileupSummaries \
    -I tumor.bam \
    -V small_exac_common.vcf.gz \
    -L targets.interval_list \
    -O tumor_pileups.table

gatk GetPileupSummaries \
    -I normal.bam \
    -V small_exac_common.vcf.gz \
    -L targets.interval_list \
    -O normal_pileups.table

gatk CalculateContamination \
    -I tumor_pileups.table \
    -matched normal_pileups.table \
    -O contamination.table \
    --tumor-segmentation segments.table

# Filter calls
gatk FilterMutectCalls \
    -R reference.fa \
    -V tumor_vs_normal.vcf.gz \
    --contamination-table contamination.table \
    --tumor-segmentation segments.table \
    --ob-priors read-orientation-model.tar.gz \
    -O tumor_vs_normal.filtered.vcf.gz
```
:::


### Strelka2 (Alternative)


::: {.cell}

```{.bash .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Configure Strelka2
configureStrelkaSomaticWorkflow.py \
    --normalBam normal.bam \
    --tumorBam tumor.bam \
    --referenceFasta reference.fa \
    --exome \
    --callRegions targets.bed.gz \
    --runDir strelka_run

# Run workflow
strelka_run/runWorkflow.py -m local -j 16

# Results in:
# strelka_run/results/variants/somatic.snvs.vcf.gz
# strelka_run/results/variants/somatic.indels.vcf.gz
```
:::


## Variant Filtering

### Hard Filtering (for small datasets)


::: {.cell}

```{.bash .cell-code}
# SNP filters
gatk VariantFiltration \
    -R reference.fa \
    -V cohort.vcf.gz \
    -O cohort.filtered.vcf.gz \
    --filter-name "QD_filter" --filter-expression "QD < 2.0" \
    --filter-name "FS_filter" --filter-expression "FS > 60.0" \
    --filter-name "MQ_filter" --filter-expression "MQ < 40.0" \
    --filter-name "SOR_filter" --filter-expression "SOR > 3.0" \
    --filter-name "MQRankSum_filter" --filter-expression "MQRankSum < -12.5" \
    --filter-name "ReadPosRankSum_filter" --filter-expression "ReadPosRankSum < -8.0"
```
:::


## Variant Statistics


::: {.cell}

```{.bash .cell-code}
# Basic stats with bcftools
bcftools stats cohort.filtered.vcf.gz > cohort.stats.txt

# Ti/Tv ratio
bcftools stats cohort.filtered.vcf.gz | grep "TSTV"

# Variants per sample
bcftools query -f '%CHROM\t%POS\t%REF\t%ALT[\t%SAMPLE=%GT]\n' cohort.filtered.vcf.gz
```
:::


## R Analysis of Variant Calls


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
library(VariantAnnotation)
library(tidyverse)

# Read VCF
vcf <- readVcf("somatic.filtered.vcf.gz", "hg38")

# Extract variant information
variants <- rowRanges(vcf)

# Get genotype info
gt <- geno(vcf)$GT

# Summarize
tibble(
  chrom = as.character(seqnames(variants)),
  pos = start(variants),
  ref = as.character(ref(variants)),
  alt = as.character(unlist(alt(variants)))
) |>
  count(chrom) |>
  arrange(desc(n))
```
:::


## Next Steps

With variant calls, proceed to:
- [CNV Calling](03-cnv-calling.qmd) for copy number analysis
- [Annotation](04-annotation.qmd) for functional interpretation
